Beneath the curved toadstool cap, raindrops drum overhead like thunder on a vast umbrella roof.
Atop the sunflower's golden platform, she surveys the garden jungle stretching endlessly below her feet.
Inside the acorn shell, spider silk tapestries shimmer against soft moss walls in this perfect chamber.
Each foxglove bell a spotted cathedral she flies through, purple chambers hanging like vertical hallways overhead.
The lily pad deck rocks gently beneath her, dragonflies crossing above like iridescent airships on patrol.
Woven twigs tower around her in the abandoned nest, now a cozy multi-room dwelling among branches.
Threading between thyme and rosemary stalks, each herb plant a fragrant tree in this windowsill forest.
Dandelion parachutes tower overhead like translucent parasols, each seed pod taller than her entire body.
The mossy log stretches like a fallen skyscraper, bracket fungi forming perfect ledges up its side.
Rainbow light fractures through the glass jar walls, turning her shelter into a glowing crystal palace.
Dawn's spiderweb holds dewdrops bigger than her head, each a perfect sphere reflecting the world.
Wild strawberries loom like boulders around her, their red surfaces dotted with seed craters she could climb.
A teacup forgotten in grass becomes a porcelain tower, its curved interior echoing with her voice.
Rose thorns jut out like dangerous spikes along the massive stem highway she carefully navigates upward.
The lavender stalk sways like a skyscraper in wind, purple flowers clustered at its towering peak.
Clover blooms form a pink canopy overhead, each petal soft as fabric in this miniature meadow.
Inside the seashell, spiral chambers lead inward, her footsteps echoing in the pearl-smooth corridors within.
Blades of grass rise like green towers, morning dew sliding down in streams she must dodge.
The honeycomb structure provides hexagonal rooms, abandoned wax chambers perfect for a temporary home tonight.
Fern fronds unfurl above like massive feathered umbrellas, creating a layered emerald ceiling of shade.
A keyhole becomes a doorway in the garden shed, splinters of wood like beams framing her entrance.
Buttercup petals glow like golden satellite dishes around her, each flower a reflective bowl of light.
The pinecone's scales create a spiraling staircase, each wooden layer a landing in this botanical tower.
Between pebbles she squeezes through canyons, each stone a boulder blocking paths through the garden.
Mint leaves spread overhead like great green platforms, their powerful scent overwhelming in this jungle density.
